Description: Gillmeria ochrodactyla genome assembly, chromosome: 14
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Lepidoptera; Glossata; Ditrysia; Pterophoroidea; Pterophoridae; Pterophorinae; Gillmeria
Assembly: The assembly ilGilOchr1.hap1.1 is based on 28x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome Z was identified through the detection of ancestral BUSCO genes.

Taxonomy_x: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Lepidoptera; Glossata; Ditrysia; Pterophoroidea; Pterophoridae; Pterophorinae; Gillmeria

Assembly: The assembly ilGilPall1.hap1.1 is based on 27x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Z chromosome identified based on synteny with Stenoptilia bipunctidactyla (GCA_944452665.1).

Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Spiralia; Lophotrochozoa; Mollusca; Gastropoda; Vetigastropoda; Trochida; Trochoidea; Trochidae; Cantharidinae; Steromphala
Assembly: The assembly of Steromphala pennanti (xgStePenn6.1) is based on 71X of PacBio data and 162X of Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 67 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial), totaling 11.66 Mb (with the largest being 7.95 Mb). Additionally, 272 regions totaling 49.16 Mb (with the largest being 6.07 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 5 haplotypic regions and and 4 contaminant sequences were removed, totaling 4.29 Mb and 0.31 Mb, respectively (with the largest being 1.47 Mb and 0.11 Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly mLutLut1.2 is based on 63x PacBio data, 58x 10X Genomics Chromium data, BioNano data and 17x Hi-C data generated at the Baylor College of Medicine. The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Falcon-unzip, retained haplotig identification with Purge Haplotigs, 10X based scaffolding with scaff10x, Hi-C based scaffolding and improvement by the Aiden lab as part of a collaboration with DNA Zoo using 3D-DNA and Juicebox, Arrow polishing, and two rounds of FreeBayes polishing. The mitochondrial assembly was produced at The Rockefeller University using mitoVGP. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Chordata; Craniata; Vertebrata; Euteleostomi; Mammalia; Eutheria; Laurasiatheria; Carnivora; Caniformia; Musteloidea; Mustelidae; Mustelinae; Mustela
Assembly: The assembly mMusErm2.hap2.1 is based on 27x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. The X and Y chromosome was identified by copy number in assembly. Multiple rearrangements (inversions, translocations) was observed in the region on chromosome 10 (92.9-113.2 Mbp). The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome X (78.3 - 86.9 Mbp) are unknown. A large number of repeats is in the unlocalised sequences.
